book_title: 'Recipes I Am No Longer Allowed to Make'
monument_name: an equestrian statue, no horse, long story
quirk_hist: has '{qfig}' scratched into it in a shaky hand
has a tooth stuck in it. {qbeast}-sized, an expert would say
smells faintly of the quarantine vinegar they used during {qplague}
is dated 'year {qyear}' by the maker's mark
was clearly salvaged from the ashes of {qsite}
has '{qfig} owes me' carved on the underside
is wrapped in a page torn from {qbook}
rattles like it swallowed something during {qplague}
carries a museum tag: 'recovered near {qsite}, do not lick'
bears scorch marks in the shape of {qfaction}'s old banner
has been chewed and, judging by the pattern, apologized to
still has a {qfaction} requisition slip glued to it
is engraved 'IN MEMORY OF {qfig}, WHO SHOULD HAVE DUCKED'
hums a tune old-timers swear was popular around year {qyear}
vendor_line: Everything's for sale. Some of it's even mine.
Bought from adventurers. Sold to adventurers. The circle of life.
No refunds. The last person who asked is a cautionary tale now.
Half this stock fell off a wagon. The wagon also for sale.
You break it, you bought it. You already touched it, so.
Finest goods this side of the incident.
I appraise with my heart, which is why the prices look like that.
Everything here has a story. Most of them end in a shrug.
chron_faction_founded: {faction} was founded in year {year}, in {extra}. It seemed like a good idea at the time.
In year {year}, {faction} first raised its banner over {extra}.
Year {year}: {faction} formed in {extra}, mostly out of spite.
{faction} traces its charter to year {year} and a handshake nobody honored.
chron_figure_rise: {actor}, {trait} {profession} of {faction}, first made a name in year {year}.
In year {year}, people started telling stories about {actor}. Most were true.
Year {year}: {faction} gained {actor}, famously {trait}. Time would tell.
{actor} arrived in year {year} with a reputation and a plan, in that order.
chron_artifact_forged: In year {year}, {actor} forged {artifact} and left it at {site}. {motive}
{artifact} left {actor}'s forge in year {year}. Everyone at {site} heard about it. Repeatedly.
Year {year}: {actor} made {artifact}. The forge never quite cooled after.
{actor} finished {artifact} in year {year} and immediately regretted showing anyone.
motive: They said it was for a rival. It was.
Officially, a gift. Unofficially, a threat.
Nobody asked why. That was the mistake.
It was meant to settle a debt. It settled several people instead.
Commissioned by someone who never came back for it.
The design came to them in a dream, reportedly a rude one.
Made to win back an old flame. It did not, but it could split shields.
The smith called it retirement planning.
Forged on a bet with a priest. The priest paid up.
chron_war_declared: In year {year}, {faction} declared war on {faction2} over {war_reason}.
Year {year}: {faction} and {faction2} went to war. The cause was {war_reason}.
The heralds of {faction} read the declaration against {faction2} in year {year}. The stated grievance: {war_reason}.
{faction} vs {faction2}, year {year}. Historians agree it was about {war_reason}, and also money.
war_reason: a border drawn in fading ink
a duel that got out of hand
the naming rights to a river
a diplomatic gift of visibly used socks
an inheritance dispute involving a parrot
a play that portrayed the wrong people accurately
the theft of a beloved municipal bell
chron_city_sacked: {site} burned in year {year}. {faction} called it diplomacy.
In year {year}, {faction} sacked {site}. The survivors opened a very bitter tavern.
Year {year}: {site} fell to {faction}. The looting was described as 'thorough' and 'frankly rude.'
{faction} put {site} to the torch in year {year}. The insurance dispute continues.
chron_peace_signed: The war between {faction} and {faction2} ended in year {year}. Nobody remembers who won.
Peace, year {year}: {faction} and {faction2} agreed to hate each other quietly.
In year {year}, {faction} and {faction2} signed a peace both sides describe as temporary.
The treaty of year {year} ended hostilities between {faction} and {faction2}. The wording contains a soup clause.
chron_beast_slain: In year {year}, {actor} slew {beast}. The ballads exaggerate, but not by much.
{beast} died in year {year}, at the hands of {actor}, who never shut up about it.
Year {year}: {actor} did what three armies couldn't and killed {beast}, mostly by accident.
{actor} felled {beast} in year {year}. The taxidermy bill bankrupted a village.
chron_figure_eaten: {beast} ate {actor} in year {year}. The beast reports no regrets.
In year {year}, {actor} discovered {beast} was not, in fact, asleep.
Year {year}: {actor} challenged {beast}. The beast accepted. Briefly.
{actor}'s last words in year {year} were 'watch this.' {beast} watched.
chron_artifact_slaying: In year {year}, {actor} took up {artifact} and ended {beast}. The artifact remembers.
Year {year}: armed with {artifact}, {actor} slew {beast}. Both are in the ballads now.
{actor} borrowed {artifact} in year {year} and returned it covered in {beast}.
chron_book_written: In year {year}, {actor} published {extra}. Critics called it 'a book.'
{extra}, by {actor}, appeared in year {year}. It remains required reading somewhere, as punishment.
Year {year}: {actor} finished {extra}. The footnotes started two feuds.
chron_trade_pact: {faction} and {faction2} signed a trade pact in year {year}. Prices rose anyway.
Year {year}: {faction} and {faction2} agreed to overcharge each other in a spirit of friendship.
The trade accord of year {year} bound {faction} and {faction2} in commerce and mutual suspicion.
In year {year}, {faction} and {faction2} opened their roads to each other. Highwaymen rejoiced.
chron_cult_founded: In year {year}, {actor} founded {extra}. Membership includes robes.
{extra} first gathered in year {year}, led by {actor}, who had been acting weird for a while.
Year {year}: {actor} started {extra}. It began as a book club. They always do.
{extra} emerged in year {year}. {actor} insists it is 'not a cult,' which is what cults say.
chron_artifact_stolen: {artifact} vanished in year {year}. {actor} was coincidentally rich that season.
In year {year}, {actor} 'relocated' {artifact}. It rests now at {site}, officially by accident.
Year {year}: {artifact} was stolen with such artistry that {actor} was almost admired for it.
{actor} liberated {artifact} in year {year}, citing finders-keepers, an ancient law.
chron_plague: {extra} swept the land in year {year}. Handshakes went out of fashion.
Year {year}: {extra}. The physicians recommended vinegar and distance.
In year {year}, {extra} arrived by ship, cart, and hug.
{extra} struck in year {year}. Onions were involved in every proposed cure.
chron_sky_debris: In year {year}, something fell screaming from the sky and became {site}. The debris still blinks.
Year {year}: the stars dropped something. {site} marks where the world flinched.
A light crossed the sky in year {year} and stopped abruptly at {site}. Salvagers still argue jurisdiction.
In year {year}, the heavens littered. {site} is the fine.
chron_figure_died: {actor} died in year {year} of natural causes, allegedly.
In year {year}, {actor} retired permanently from being alive.
Year {year}: {actor} died as they lived - mid-argument.
{actor} passed in year {year}, leaving debts, grudges, and one genuinely nice memory.
chron_duel_fought: In year {year}, {actor} dueled {extra} over a matter both called honor and neither could explain. {extra} lost.
Year {year}: {actor} and {extra} settled it at dawn. {extra} is the reason we say 'settled.'
{actor} won a duel against {extra} in year {year}. Witnesses describe the winning move as 'legally ambiguous.'
chron_figure_exiled: In year {year}, {faction} exiled {actor}, who joined {faction2} out of spite, their primary skill.
Year {year}: {actor} wore out their welcome with {faction} and defected to {faction2}.
{faction} formally banished {actor} in year {year}. {faction2} took them in and regretted it within the season.
chron_monument_built: In year {year}, {faction} unveiled {extra}. It leans, but respectfully.
Year {year}: {faction} completed {extra}, over budget and under level.
{faction} raised {extra} in year {year} to commemorate something no one now recalls.
epitaph: Died as they lived: confused.
The paperwork was fatal.
Peer pressure, mostly.
In hindsight, an avoidable death.
They died doing what they loved. Unfortunately.
Local wildlife remains unapologetic.
Gravity was involved.
An honest mistake, repeated four times.
The gods watched. They did not help. They took notes.
Cause of death: optimism.
The sign was right there.
Survived by their debts.
A tactical retreat, executed in the wrong direction.
The mushroom was lying.
Correct about everything except the important thing.
Died haggling. The vendor kept the difference.
The plan had exactly one flaw. This was it.
Should have taken the bread.
The floor was load-bearing after all. The ceiling wasn't.
Trusted a rock labeled RULES.
The last words were 'it's probably fine.'
Not the first to die this way, which is somehow worse.
Died rich in experience, poor in everything else.
The tavern is naming a sandwich after the incident.
Slain by a technicality.
The dice were fair. That was the problem.
Went back for the loot.
A hero, briefly.
The curse was real. The refund was not.
Nobody checked on them. They were the one who usually checked.
